notes.introduction: A Wednesday evening expository lesson (number eight in a series) on 1 Thessalonians 5:15, examining the command to refrain from returning evil for evil and to pursue good, with Romans 12:17-21 as a companion passage.
outline: I. A Principle of Grace: the command 'see that none render evil for evil unto any man,' with attention to the word 'see' as 'take heed' and the natural fleshly desire for retaliation
II. A Pursuit of Good: the command to 'ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves and to all men,' with the standard of good defined by God rather than culture or society
themes: the local church and christian distinctiveness
